lede: A look at August 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including François Hollande and Djibril Cissé, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Plymouth
events.desc: Six people, five victims and the perpetrator are killed in Keyham, Plymouth in the worst mass shooting in the UK since 2010.

events.title: Japan Air Lines Flight 123
events.desc: Japan Air Lines Flight 123, a Boeing 747, crashes into Osutaka ridge in Gunma Prefecture, Japan, killing 520, becoming the worst single-plane air disaster.

events.title: South Africa
events.desc: South Africa is banned from the Olympic Games due to the country's racist policies.

events.title: Night of the Murdered Poets
events.desc: The Night of the Murdered Poets: Thirteen prominent Jewish intellectuals are murdered in Moscow, Russia, Soviet Union.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: Bloody Gulch massacre: Seventy-five American POWs are massacred by the North Korean Army.

events.title: Nazi Germany
events.desc: Nazi German troops end the week-long Wola massacre, during which time at least 40,000 people are killed indiscriminately or in mass executions.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: France, the United Kingdom and the British Empire declare war on Austria-Hungary.

events.title: Quagga
events.desc: The last quagga dies at the Natura Artis Magistra, a zoo in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

events.title: Rhône (department)
events.desc: The Rhône and Loire départments are created when the former département of Rhône-et-Loire is split into two.

events.title: Christopher Columbus
events.desc: Christopher Columbus arrives in the Canary Islands on his first voyage to the New World.

births.name: François Hollande
births.line: French lawyer and politician, 24th President of France

births.name: Erwin Schrödinger
births.line: Austrian physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: George Soros
births.line: Hungarian-American businessman and investor, founded the Soros Fund Management

deaths.text: Thomas Mann, German author and critic, Nobel Prize laureate
Yongle Emperor, emperor of the Ming Empire

observances.text: Christian feast day: Jane Frances de Chantal
